data.narrative:
Eikon Therapeutics is a Series B biotech company founded by Luke Lavis, Eric Betzig, Robert Tjian, and Xavier Darzacq, currently valued at $3.3B post-money with $700M+ raised to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies in Biotech & Health, with 4 direct same-stage comparables used in quantitative methods.

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $3.3B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a forward valuation of $6.3B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage companies—Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median valuation of $805M. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied, as target revenue is not disclosed.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $684M to $7.2B, with a median of $3.5B. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between peer-median positioning (substantially below current valuation) and stage-progression methodology (substantially above). Key uncertainties include the accuracy of the stage-progression multiplier for this specific sector context and whether the peer set adequately reflects Eikon's differentiated technology or capital efficiency profile.
